keyPoints: Encapsulation: Bundling data and methods that operate on that data within one unit (class), and restricting access to some of the object's components.
Inheritance: Allows a new class (subclass) to inherit the properties and behaviors (methods) of an existing class (superclass), promoting code reusability.

Polymorphism: Allows objects of different classes to be treated as objects of a common super class, typically through method overriding, enabling one interface to be used for a general class of actions.
commonMistakes: Confusing Inheritance (is-a relationship) with Composition (has-a relationship).
Forgetting to use the 'private' access modifier to properly achieve Encapsulation, leaving data fields exposed.
Attempting to override a static or final method, which is not allowed and misunderstands Polymorphism.
